title: Allow relative paths in org-mcp-allowed-files
description: Match org-agenda-files behavior: relative paths in org-mcp-allowed-files are resolved against org-directory, so absolute and relative path entries are interchangeable.
close_reason: Implemented: org-mcp-allowed-files now accepts relative paths (resolved against org-directory, matching org-agenda-files). 8 new tests added; full suite 255/255 green; README updated.

title: Remove stored org-mcp queries feature
description: Drop stored-queries endpoints (list/save/delete/run-stored-query) and the org-mcp-stored-queries-file defcustom. Keep statically-defined query-* endpoints. Update README.

title: Use canonical visited buffers for org-mcp file operations
description: Why this issue exists and what needs to be done: org-mcp currently reads and writes Org files through temp buffers and fails when a visited buffer has unsaved changes. Switch Org file operations to canonical visited buffers so MCP tools can see and update the same buffer Emacs uses. Preserve user editing state, avoid autosaving pre-modified buffers, save clean or newly opened buffers, align clocking semantics, and document the behavior.
design: Refactor shared file/buffer helpers so mutation logic runs once inside with-current-buffer on the canonical buffer from find-buffer-visiting/find-file-noselect. Finalization decides whether to save based on the buffer's pre-write modified state and refreshes only other visiting buffers after disk writes.
acceptance_criteria: Org tools operate on canonical visited buffers; pre-modified buffers are edited without auto-save; clean/newly opened buffers are saved after edit; read paths see unsaved buffer content; clocking follows the same save semantics; README documents the behavior.
